content: {"perspective": "Scientific", "question": "Round 2 established that protocol type, intervention duration, sex, PA, and BMI are the dominant moderators of IF effect heterogeneity, but no snippet reported the specific I² reduction after adjustment. Two deeper methodological concerns remain: (1) risk of publication bias — are the funnel-plot/Egger's test results across the major MetS-IF meta-analyses consistent with an unbiased literature, and has any trim-and-fill or PET-PEESE correction been applied? (2) Reconciliation of the Ramadan free-living observational literature (which shows modest, reversible benefits in Muslim populations) with the supervised-RCT literature (which shows larger effects in short-term trials): is the RCT effect size inflated by intensive monitoring and selected populations, or is the Ramadan effect size deflated by behavioral confounders and measurement noise?", "search_queries": ["intermittent fasting meta-analysis Egger test funnel plot publication bias trim fill", "intermittent fasting RCT Ramadan observational effect size discrepancy reconciliation", "PET-PEESE correction intermittent fasting metabolic meta-analysis selection bias", "Ramadan fasting metabolic syndrome RCT vs observational effect size heterogeneity", "intermittent fasting small-study effect publication bias sensitivity analysis"]}
